class.name: OutputPanel
class.brief_description: An in-game output log control for runtime debugging.
class.description: A customizable [Control] node that captures and displays engine log output directly inside a running game. It intercepts all messages produced by [method @GlobalScope.print], [method @GlobalScope.print_rich], [method @GlobalScope.printerr], [method @GlobalScope.push_error], and [method @GlobalScope.push_warning], and renders them in a scrollable panel with color-coded text. By default, [member debug_only] is [code]true[/code], which means the node is only visible and registers its log handlers in debug builds ([code]DEBUG_ENABLED[/code]). Set [member debug_only] to [code]false[/code] to keep the panel visible and active in release exports as well. [b]Note:[/b] [OutputPanel] does not capture output produced by editor operations. Handlers are registered only when the game is actually running, not while the scene is open in the editor. [b]Note:[/b] Messages are stored internally up to [member max_lines] entries. Once the limit is reached, the oldest entries are discarded to make room for new ones.

method.name: clear
method.description: Clears all messages from the panel and resets the internal message store.

members.member: If [code]true[/code], the panel is only visible and captures log output in debug builds. In release exports the handlers are never registered and the panel remains invisible and empty. If [code]false[/code], the panel is active and visible in both debug and release builds. [b]Note:[/b] Changing this property at runtime after the node has entered the scene tree has no effect. The value is read once during [constant Node.NOTIFICATION_READY].

The maximum number of log lines retained by the panel. When new messages arrive and this limit is exceeded, the oldest lines are removed first. Lowering this value on a panel that already contains more lines than the new limit will immediately discard the excess from the top.
If [code]true[/code], error messages produced by [method @GlobalScope.push_error] and [method @GlobalScope.printerr] are displayed.
If [code]true[/code], standard messages produced by [method @GlobalScope.print] and [method @GlobalScope.print_rich] are displayed.
If [code]true[/code], warning messages produced by [method @GlobalScope.push_warning] are displayed.

constants.constant: A standard plain-text message, produced by [method @GlobalScope.print].
A rich-text message that supports BBCode, produced by [method @GlobalScope.print_rich].
An error message, produced by [method @GlobalScope.push_error] or [method @GlobalScope.printerr].

theme_items.theme_item: The color used for error messages, including those produced by [method @GlobalScope.printerr].
The color used for standard messages produced by [method @GlobalScope.print].
The color used for warning messages produced by [method @GlobalScope.push_warning].
The font used for bold text, such as the [code]WARNING:[/code] and [code]ERROR:[/code] prefixes. When not set, the default theme font is used.
The font used for standard log text. When not set, the default theme font is used.
The font size used for all log text. When not set, the default theme font size is used.
The [StyleBox] drawn as the panel background.
